req.title: Export employee directory — variant 4
req.statement: An hr-admin shall export the employee directory as CSV with PII redacted for employees who opted out of public directory. Additionally, the operation must be rate-limited per user.

doc.reasoning: The requirement has four testable dimensions: (1) happy-path CSV export with PII redaction for opted-out employees (EP positive), (2) 403 rejection for non-hr-admin callers (NEG), (3) 429 rate-limit enforcement at the exact request boundary (BVA), and (4) decision-table coverage of the opt-out flag combinations driving PII visibility (DT). Together these cover the stated error pathways, the redaction invariant, and the rate-limit constraint while remaining self-contained and idempotent through fixture-based state setup and teardown.

tests.name: test_hr_admin_export_redacts_opted_out_employee_pii
preconditions: At least one employee has opted_out=true
At least one employee has opted_out=false
actions: Identify rows for opted-out vs opted-in employees
expected: Opted-in employee rows contain full PII fields (email, phone)
Opted-out employee rows have PII fields replaced with REDACTED sentinel
tests.executable:
import csv, io
import pytest

def test_hr_admin_export_redacts_opted_out_employee_pii(
    <hr_admin_token_fixture>,
    <seed_employees_fixture>,  # seeds opted_in_id, opted_out_id
    <api_client_fixture>
):
    resp = <api_client_fixture>.get(
        "/api/employees/export.csv",
        headers={"Authorization": f"Bearer {<hr_admin_token_fixture>}"}
    )
    assert resp.status_code == 200
    assert "text/csv" in resp.headers.get("Content-Type", "")

    reader = csv.DictReader(io.StringIO(resp.text))
    rows = {r["id"]: r for r in reader}

    opted_in_id = str(<seed_employees_fixture>["opted_in_id"])
    opted_out_id = str(<seed_employees_fixture>["opted_out_id"])

    assert rows[opted_in_id]["email"] != "REDACTED"
    assert rows[opted_in_id]["phone"] != "REDACTED"
    assert rows[opted_out_id]["email"] == "REDACTED"
    assert rows[opted_out_id]["phone"] == "REDACTED"

tests.name: test_non_hr_admin_export_returns_403
expected: Response body contains error indicating forbidden/insufficient role

tests.name: test_rate_limit_triggers_on_exact_boundary_request
preconditions: Rate-limit counter for this user reset to zero (fresh window)
Rate limit is known constant RATE_LIMIT_MAX (e.g. 5 per window)
actions: Send RATE_LIMIT_MAX requests sequentially; expect 200 each
Send one additional (RATE_LIMIT_MAX + 1) request in the same window
expected: First RATE_LIMIT_MAX requests each return HTTP 200
tests.executable:
import pytest

RATE_LIMIT_MAX = <rate_limit_constant>  # e.g. 5

def test_rate_limit_triggers_on_exact_boundary_request(
    <hr_admin_token_fixture>,
    <api_client_fixture>,
    <reset_rate_limit_fixture>  # resets counter for this user before test
):
    headers = {"Authorization": f"Bearer {<hr_admin_token_fixture>}"}

    for i in range(1, RATE_LIMIT_MAX + 1):
        r = <api_client_fixture>.get("/api/employees/export.csv", headers=headers)
        assert r.status_code == 200, f"Request {i} expected 200, got {r.status_code}"

    over_limit = <api_client_fixture>.get("/api/employees/export.csv", headers=headers)
    assert over_limit.status_code == 429
    assert "Retry-After" in over_limit.headers

tests.name: test_opt_out_flag_combinations_drive_correct_pii_visibility
preconditions: Four employees seeded: opted_out=false active, opted_out=true active, opted_out=false inactive, opted_out=true inactive
actions: Parse CSV rows for all four employee variants
Assert PII visibility per decision-table rule
expected: opted_out=false, inactive => email/phone visible (status does not affect redaction)
tests.executable:
import csv, io
import pytest

@pytest.mark.parametrize("opted_out,active,expect_redacted", [
    (False, True,  False),
    (True,  True,  True),
    (False, False, False),
    (True,  False, True),
])
def test_opt_out_flag_combinations_drive_correct_pii_visibility(
    opted_out, active, expect_redacted,
    <hr_admin_token_fixture>,
    <api_client_fixture>,
    <seed_single_employee_fixture>  # returns employee id for given params
):
    emp_id = str(<seed_single_employee_fixture>(opted_out=opted_out, active=active))
    headers = {"Authorization": f"Bearer {<hr_admin_token_fixture>}"}
    resp = <api_client_fixture>.get("/api/employees/export.csv", headers=headers)
    assert resp.status_code == 200
    reader = csv.DictReader(io.StringIO(resp.text))
    rows = {r["id"]: r for r in reader}
    assert emp_id in rows, f"Employee {emp_id} missing from export"
    row = rows[emp_id]
    if expect_redacted:
        assert row["email"] == "REDACTED"
        assert row["phone"] == "REDACTED"
    else:
        assert row["email"] != "REDACTED"
        assert row["phone"] != "REDACTED"
